entries.definition: A journey or excursion, especially a short one for pleasure or business.
entries.exampleSentence: They're planning a trip to the coast next weekend.

entries.definition: To stumble or catch one's foot so that one nearly falls.
entries.exampleSentence: Be careful not to trip on that step.

entries.definition: To cause someone to stumble; to cause to fall or to cause a mechanism to activate (e.g., trip a switch).
entries.exampleSentence: The loose cable tripped him as he walked by. / The alarm was tripped when the door opened.
